name: m16-selected-gemv-grid-occupancy
purpose: Assess whether the verify-path selected GEMV grids (gate_up/down, W4) are genuine over-launch (reducible like GDN) or occupancy/structure-driven. Source: launch_selected_dual_pack8 / launch_selected_pack8 in paro_awq_gemv.hip + on-model rocprof kernel trace.
model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-PARO MTP-BF16 (hidden=2048, moe_intermediate=512, num_experts=256, top_k=8, group_size=128)
grid_structure: selected_* launchers use dim3(out_packed[_a+_b], rows); one block per unique (output_pack, row). No split-K; in_features looped internally per block. No redundant recompute across blocks.

occupancy.basis: 96 CUs x ~14 WGs/CU at VGPR=104 (~half of the 32-wave/CU cap); WG=64 threads = 2 wave32/WG

finding: NOT over-launch (every block does unique output x row work, no split-K, no redundant recompute -- categorically unlike GDN where 128 dv-blocks redid identical transcendentals). BUT large occupancy SLACK: grids run 49x-97x past the machine-fill threshold, far beyond latency-hiding need. The reduction lever is therefore output-tiling (multiple output packs/rows per block -> cheaper dispatch class + fewer x re-loads), which is a KERNEL RESTRUCTURE with a dispatch-vs-kernel-time tradeoff and B4 coalescing risk -- not a free grid collapse. Belongs with the gate_up/down kernel-time optimization (task #97), measured per-shape.
doc_correction: W7900 is 96 CUs (48 WGPs); MEGAKERNEL/ROOFLINE 'fill 48 CUs' conflated WGP with CU. Noted in MEGAKERNEL §9.3.
conclusion: Task #96 closed as analysis: selected GEMV grids are occupancy-slack output-tiling candidates, not GDN-style redundant over-launch. No standalone prototype; folded into #97.
